perturbation.original_text: shall be governed by and enforceable under, the laws of the State of New York.
perturbation.changed_text: shall be governed by and enforceable under, the laws of Canada.
perturbation.explanation: The contract initially states New York law governs the agreement. Changing this to Canadian law removes certain consumer protections afforded under New York law, specifically those related to data security and breach notification.
perturbation.law_explanation: NY GBL § 899-AA mandates specific data breach notification requirements for businesses dealing with private information of New York residents. By changing the governing law to Canada, the contract removes the protection of this law for Illinois Central Corp, potentially violating New York consumer rights.
perturbation.justification: The change from New York law to Canadian law removes the data breach notification requirements mandated by NY GBL § 899-AA, as explained in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1, referencing NY Senate.gov and General Business Article 39-F, confirms the existence of data security protections and notification requirements under New York law. By switching to Canadian law, the contract omits a legally mandated consumer protection afforded by New York law, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Recipient and that Disclosing Party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctions and orders for specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach. Such remedy shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedy for breach of this Agreement but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that money damages shall be the sole and exclusive remedy for any breach of this Agreement by Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for equitable relief (injunctions, specific performance). The modified text limits the remedy to only money damages. This could contradict the availability of injunctive relief under Illinois law for trade secret misappropriation or breach of contract where monetary damages are inadequate.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Illinois Code of Civil Procedure, 735 ILCS 5/11-101
perturbation.law_explanation: 735 ILCS 5/11-101 generally allows for injunctive relief where monetary damages are insufficient. By restricting the remedy solely to money damages, the modified text attempts to contractually limit the court's ability to grant an injunction, potentially contradicting this legal principle, especially in cases involving irreparable harm like trade secret misappropriation.

Because the statute database is maintained primarily for legislative drafting purposes, statutory changes are sometimes included in the statute database before they take effect. If the source note at the end of a Section of the statutes includes a Public Act that has not yet taken effect, the version of the law that is currently in effect may have already been removed from the database and you should refer to that Public Act to see the changes made to the current law. 735 ILCS 5/11-101 (735 ILCS 5/11-101) (from Ch. 110, par. 11-101) Sec. 11-101. Temporary restraining order. No temporary restraining order shall be granted without notice to the adverse party unless it clearly appears from specific facts shown by affidavit or by the verified complaint that immediate and irreparable injury, loss, or damage will result to the applicant before notice can be served and a hearing had thereon. Every temporary restraining order granted without notice shall be indorsed with the date and hour of signing; shall be filed forthwith in the clerk's office; shall define the injury and state why it is irreparable and why the order was granted without notice; and shall expire by its terms within such time after the signing of the order, not to exceed 10 days, as the court fixes, unless within the time so fixed the order, for good cause shown, is extended for a like period or unless the party against whom the order is directed consents that it may be extended for a longer period. The reasons for the granting of the extension shall be stated in the written order of the court. In case a temporary restraining order is granted without notice, the motion for a preliminary injunction shall be set for hearing at the earliest possible time and takes precedence over all matters except older matters of the same character; and when the motion comes on for hearing the party who obtained the temporary restraining order shall proceed with the application for a preliminary injunction and, if he or she does not do so, the court shall dissolve the temporary restraining order. On 2 days' notice to the party who obtained the temporary restraining order without notice or on such shorter notice to that party as the court may prescribe, the adverse party may appear and move its dissolution or modification and in that event the court shall proceed to hear and determine such motion as expeditiously as the ends of justice require. Every order granting an injunction and every restraining orde
perturbation.justification: The modified text limits remedies to money damages, while the original text allows for equitable relief, including injunctions. The Law Explanation states that 735 ILCS 5/11-101 allows for injunctive relief where monetary damages are insufficient. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 735 ILCS 5/11-101, discusses temporary restraining orders and injunctions, implying the availability of such remedies. By restricting the remedy solely to money damages, the modified text attempts to contractually limit the court's ability to grant an injunction, contradicting this legal principle.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions of this Agreement pertaining to Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement shall continue and survive for a period of three (3) years after the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions of this Agreement pertaining to Proprietary Information disclosed under this Agreement shall terminate immediately upon the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause has a survival period of three years for confidentiality obligations. The modified clause eliminates this survival period. This could be problematic because trade secret protection can extend beyond contract termination, and immediate termination might violate protections afforded under the Illinois Trade Secrets Act.
perturbation.law_explanation: 765 ILCS 1065/8(b)(1) allows for injunctive relief against misappropriation of trade secrets. If the agreement terminates immediately, there is no contractual obligation to maintain confidentiality, potentially allowing for misappropriation without contractual recourse immediately after the agreement ends, thus diminishing protections offered by the Illinois Trade Secrets Act.

perturbation.justification: The changed text eliminates the three-year survival period for confidentiality obligations related to Proprietary Information, which contradicts the protections afforded by the Illinois Trade Secrets Act. The Law Explanation notes that 765 ILCS 1065/8(b)(1) allows for injunctive relief against misappropriation of trade secrets. By terminating confidentiality obligations immediately upon termination of the agreement, the modified clause potentially allows for misappropriation without contractual recourse, thereby weakening protections offered by the Act. The Perturbation Explanation also highlights this potential violation. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not available, the Law Explanation sufficiently indicates a legal contradiction based on the Illinois Trade Secrets Act.
